destinations.evidence: Payload building now applies the global deadline before scheduled obligation/predicate collection, fulfilled async collection, delivery-ready collection, sequencing-rights reorder, and the execution loop.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: all expensive optional collection/reorder phases covered by this invariant now check the shared build deadline before starting and proceed toward sealing with already collected work when the deadline has elapsed.

related_but_insufficient_code: The original audit observed missing guards before scheduled, fulfilled async, and delivery-ready collection. Local port code now guards all three before entering those phases.

notes: Resolved locally by adding the missing scheduled obligation/predicate deadline guard and sharing the deadline predicate with the existing fulfilled, delivery, sequencing, and execution guards.
rationale: The merge wraps major payload collection/sort work in deadline guards, passes global_deadline into obligation computation, and logs that skipped fulfilled async, delivery settlements, or sequencing rights can be picked up or ordered by simpler fallbacks. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The exact phase boundaries and logging targets are builder implementation details; the load-bearing behavior is that payload construction does not keep doing expensive optional work after the configured budget.
statement: When the payload build deadline has already elapsed, the builder must skip remaining expensive collection, predicate filtering, fulfilled async, delivery, and sequencing-rights phases and continue toward sealing with the work already collected.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure a near-zero build deadline and a pool containing scheduled, fulfilled async, delivery, and sequencing-rights candidates; assert the builder skips deadline-guarded phases, returns a payload instead of stalling, and leaves skipped candidates for future blocks when applicable.
summary: Deadline skipping is mapped for scheduled, fulfilled async, delivery, sequencing, and execution phases.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set RETH_BUILD_DEADLINE_MS near zero and make scheduled predicates, fulfilled async, and delivery-ready jobs expensive; the mapped port path should skip deadline-guarded phases and proceed toward sealing.
title: Builder skips expensive phases after deadline
